text: The problem is matrix chain multiplication. In this video I'll explain you what is matrix multiplication problem then what does it mean by matrix chain multiplication then I'll show how to solve this by applying dynamic programming then we will come up with the formula that is used for solving this problem and also I will discuss the time complexity. So let us start with matrix multiplication. First of all if you have a matrix two matrices and you want to multiply them and their dimensions are suppose 5 x 4 and 4 x 3. The condition for multiplying two matrices is the number of columns of the first matrix should be equal to number of rows of second matrix then only multiplication can be done. If I write other way b x a then their dimensions 4 x 3 and 5 x 4 will be there they cannot be multiplied because these are not same. So a x b if it is possible then b x a is also possible it's not necessary. When you multiply the resultant matrix will be c and the dimension of that resultant matrix will be 5 x 3 number of rows of first one and number of columns of last one 5 x 3. So it means how many elements will be there so 15 elements will be there then for getting each element what we are suppose to do multiply first row with first column. So how many elements first row will have 4 elements and this will also have 4 elements that is 4 you can see. So total 4 multiplications I have to do for getting this one single element then again first row with second column I get this element so it means for getting each element of the C matrix I have to make 4 multiplications so this is into 4 so finally it is like this 5 x 3 x 4 this is 60. So it means for generating the C matrix I have to make 60 multiplications 5 4 3 60 multiplications are required. So this is about the cost of multiplying 2 matrices depends on the number of multiplications that you are performing so how to obtain the number of multiplication this how we can find out the number of multiplications. Now coming to the problem here 4 matrices are given and the dimensions are given they are arranged such that they can be multiplied because these are same you see and 6 6 is same 2 2 is same so they can be multiplied but the question is not to multiply and get the answer no we don't want the answer for multiplying I cannot multiply all of them together I have to take a pair at a time and multiply. So the question is which pair I should select such that the total cost of multiplying all of them is minimum. So the problem is not to multiply them the problem is to know how to multiply them.
